1	---
2	ticker: META
3	last_updated: 2026-05-18
4	base_wacc: 9.0
5	growth_ceiling: 0.18
6	terminal_margin: 0.28
7	confidence_anchor: HIGH
8	bear_narrative: "Reality Labs cash drain intensifies; regulatory antitrust action (EU structural remedy or US Instagram/WhatsApp dissolution) forces business separation; AI ad-targeting fails to improve ROAS meaningfully in a macro downturn — Family of Apps revenue decelerates sharply while elevated capex continues"
9	base_narrative: "Family of Apps sustains mid-teens revenue growth on AI-targeting ROAS gains and Threads monetisation; Reality Labs losses stabilise below $20B/year as smart-glasses hardware gains commercial traction; regulatory fines (DMA, GDPR) remain manageable without structural remedy"
10	bull_narrative: "AI-driven ad-targeting unlocks a step-change in advertiser ROAS, re-accelerating revenue growth above consensus; Reality Labs smart-glasses pivot toward profitable hardware and enterprise AR; EU/US regulatory outcomes resolved without structural separation — regulatory overhang re-rated as a non-event"
11	failure_modes:
12	  - "DAP (Daily Active People) growth decelerates below 3% YoY — engagement floor premise breaks, monetisation leverage disappears"
13	  - "EU antitrust forced structural separation of Messenger, WhatsApp, or Instagram from the core Facebook platform"
14	  - "US DOJ action seeking dissolution of the Instagram and/or WhatsApp acquisitions"
15	  - "Reality Labs capex sustainably above $20B/year without a credible near-term monetisation path — free cash flow drag becomes consensus-thesis-breaking"
16	  - "AI-generated content floods the feed and degrades ad-targeting signal quality — privacy-sandbox-style ROAS deterioration without a regulatory catalyst"
17	  - "Threads fails to retain users at scale; no advertising revenue materialises by 2027 — eliminates the third-pillar optionality embedded in the base case"
18	---
19	
20	# META Playbook
21	
22	**Coverage status:** ESTABLISHED · INCUMBENT  
23	**Thesis in one line:** Family of Apps cash machine + Reality Labs binary option + AI ad-targeting accuracy lift, with regulatory overhang as a recurring drag but not a structural break.
24	
25	---
26	
27	## 1. Business Architecture
28	
29	### Segment mix (approximate FY2025 basis)
30	
31	| Segment | Revenue share | Operating income share | Growth trajectory |
32	|---|---|---|---|
33	| Family of Apps (FoA) — advertising | ~97% | ~110%+ (absorbs RL losses) | Mid-to-high teens |
34	| Reality Labs (RL) | ~3% | −25% (operating loss ~$17–20B/yr) | Near-zero revenue; rising investment |
35	
36	FoA advertising is the only cash-generating segment. RL is a long-duration binary bet: either smart glasses / AR / VR achieve commercial scale (2028+) or the division is wound down / spun off. The DCF base case values FoA only; RL optionality is a qualitative upside that is not modelled in the central case.
37	
38	### Key revenue drivers within FoA
39	
40	1. **DAP (Daily Active People)** — the volume metric. Consensus expects low-to-mid single-digit YoY growth from a ~3.4B base. Below 3% is a warning; above 5% is a positive surprise.
41	2. **ARPU (Average Revenue Per User)** — the monetisation metric, driven by ad price (CPMs), ad load, and AI-targeting ROAS improvements. NA/EU ARPUs are 10–20× rest-of-world; mix shift toward APAC depresses blended ARPU.
42	3. **AI ad-targeting** — Meta's AI-driven ad-targeting (Advantage+ suite) claims a measurable ROAS lift for advertisers. This is the swing factor in the base case: if advertisers continuously validate ROAS improvements, ad budgets shift toward Meta even at higher CPMs.
43	
44	---
45	
46	## 2. Capex-Cycle Position
47	
48	Meta is in an **accelerating investment phase** (2024–2027). Data-centre and AI infrastructure capex is guided to $60–65B in 2025, up from $38B in 2024. This depresses FCF margin below steady-state.
49	
50	**Normalised-margin thesis:** The `terminal_margin` override of 28% reflects a thesis-consistent steady-state SBC-stripped FCF margin once the current capex cycle digests (2027+). The TTM clean FCF margin (typically 22–25% at peak capex) understates this — hence the override prevents anchoring the DCF on a trough-margin base.
51	
52	**Risk:** If AI capex is structural and perpetual (not cyclical), 28% terminal margin never materialises. The bear case's narrative captures this via compressed Y1 anchor.
53	
54	---
55	
56	## 3. Active Catalysts
57	
58	| Catalyst | Probability | IV impact | Direction |
59	|---|---|---|---|
60	| EU DMA structural remedy (data-sharing mandate) | ~30% | −10–15% | Negative — share IV |
61	| US DOJ antitrust (Instagram/WhatsApp dissolution) | ~15% | −20–30% | Strongly negative |
62	| Reality Labs hardware breakthrough (smart glasses sell-out) | ~20% | +5–10% | Positive — optionality re-rating |
63	| AI ROAS re-acceleration above consensus | ~35% | +10–15% | Positive — bull-case trigger |
64	| Threads monetisation materially earlier than 2027 | ~25% | +3–5% | Positive — incremental |
65	| Macro recession (ad-budget freeze) | ~20% | −15–20% | Negative — all scenarios shift bear-ward |
66	
67	---
68	
69	## 4. Sell-Side Disagreement Axes
70	
71	The real arguments the sell-side is having on META right now:
72	
73	1. **Capex duration:** Is the $60B+ infrastructure spend a one-time catch-up (2024–2026) after which capex/revenue normalises ≤15%? Or is AI infrastructure a structural annual commitment that permanently compresses FCF margins? Bulls model mean-reversion; bears model a new capex regime.
74	
75	2. **AI ROAS sustainability:** Is the Advantage+ ROAS lift a durable structural advantage (AI is a moat) or a cyclical tailwind that competitors (Google, TikTok) will match within 18–24 months? Bulls model permanent monetisation acceleration; bears model CPM normalisation.
76	
77	3. **Regulatory endpoint:** Is the regulatory risk a repeated-fines tax (~2–3% of FCF/year) that the stock already prices in, or is structural separation (Instagram/WhatsApp dissolution) a non-trivial probability? The market's current implied probability for hard structural action appears to be <10%, which is where the disagreement sits.
78	
79	4. **Reality Labs terminal value:** Is RL worth zero (a recurring cash drain that should be shut down), a negative value (capex overhang reduces equity value), a call option (worth $20–50B if spatial computing achieves scale), or a strategic platform (worth $100B+ in a 10-year horizon)? This disagreement is the largest source of cross-analyst target-price dispersion.
80	
81	5. **DAP ceiling:** Does the ~3.4B DAP figure represent near-saturation in developed markets (implying ARPU must carry all growth), or is there a meaningful tail of untapped engagement in South/Southeast Asia and Africa that sustains volume growth for another 5 years?
82	
83	---
84	
85	## 5. Failure Modes
86	
87	These are the thesis-breaking scenarios that the DCF does not capture because they are discrete rather than smooth:
88	
89	- **DAP deceleration below 3% YoY** — the monetisation ARPU lever requires volume to remain relevant; sustained sub-3% DAP growth signals a platform that is mature without the ARPU ceiling to offset it.
90	- **EU structural separation** — a binding remedy requiring interoperability or data separation between Facebook/Instagram/WhatsApp would reduce the FoA bundle's CPM efficiency and potentially require structural divestitures at depressed multiples.
91	- **US acquisition reversal** — the most damaging tail risk; a forced unwinding of Instagram or WhatsApp would bifurcate cash flows, require separate capex programmes, and likely destroy synergy value.
92	- **Reality Labs structural capex** — if the $17–20B/year RL operating loss is not a cyclical investment but a permanent run-rate, steady-state FCF is systematically overstated in all scenarios.
93	- **AI-generated content flood** — if GenAI content degrades engagement quality (user fatigue, ad-adjacency concerns), the engagement-monetisation loop could weaken faster than the current data suggests.
94	
95	---
96	
97	## 6. Ticker-Specific Overrides (Rationale)
98	
99	| Field | Value | Rationale |
100	|---|---|---|
101	| `base_wacc` | 9.0% | Beta ~1.1–1.2; risk-free rate 4.2% (US 10yr); ERP ~5.5%; regulatory premium ~0.3pp. Rounds to 9.0%. |
102	| `growth_ceiling` | 18% | Consensus EPS growth estimate is typically 18–22% for META; 18% matches the lower bound of consensus and the conservative interpretation of mid-teens FoA revenue growth × operating leverage. Consensus takes precedence when available; this overrides the generic fallback. |
103	| `terminal_margin` | 28% | Capex-normalised SBC-stripped FCF margin estimate once the AI infrastructure investment cycle digests (~2027+). Current TTM clean margin (≈22–25%) is trough-distorted; anchoring Y1 FCF on the normalised margin prevents systematic understatement of the base IV during a known investment cycle. |
104	| `confidence_anchor` | HIGH | MCP data quality for META is consistently good (4y FCF history, SBC available, consensus estimates populated). No structural reason to cap below HIGH unless SBC or shares data degrades at run time. |
105	
106	---
107	
108	## 7. Historical Reset-and-Recover Priors
109	
110	| Episode | Drawdown | Catalyst | Recovery | Lesson |
111	|---|---|---|---|---|
112	| 2018 Cambridge Analytica / GDPR | −43% peak-to-trough | Privacy scandal + regulatory reaction | Full recovery in ~12 months | Regulatory events reset the stock sharply but do not break the ad-monetisation flywheel if DAP holds |
113	| 2022 "Year of Efficiency" reset | −76% peak-to-trough (2021–2022) | Reality Labs losses disclosed + DAP YoY decline (Q1 2022, first ever) + macro ad recession | Recovery to all-time highs by 2023 | When Zuckerberg pivots to cost discipline, execution follows quickly; the reset creates a trough-margin base year that inflates trailing FCF CAGRs for 2–3 years afterward (this is the ABA-111 cap rationale for META) |
114	| 2023 AI-reset rerating | +194% in calendar year | "Year of Efficiency" cost cuts + AI ad-targeting ROAS inflection + broader AI rerating | — | The stock rewards operational discipline and AI-monetisation evidence faster than consensus updates |
115	
116	**Mean-reversion implication:** META has consistently recovered from regulatory and operational resets within 12–24 months when the core DAP/ARPU flywheel remains intact. The recovery speed is higher when (a) management demonstrates cost discipline and (b) DAP does not sustain a multi-quarter decline. Bear scenarios that assume a permanent structural break (dissolution, permanent engagement loss) are qualitatively different from cyclical reset scenarios — the historical prior does not apply to a forced structural outcome.

---
ticker: META
last_updated: 2026-05-18
base_wacc: 9.0
growth_ceiling: 0.18
terminal_margin: 0.28
confidence_anchor: HIGH
bear_narrative: "Reality Labs cash drain intensifies; regulatory antitrust action (EU structural remedy or US Instagram/WhatsApp dissolution) forces business separation; AI ad-targeting fails to improve ROAS meaningfully in a macro downturn — Family of Apps revenue decelerates sharply while elevated capex continues"
base_narrative: "Family of Apps sustains mid-teens revenue growth on AI-targeting ROAS gains and Threads monetisation; Reality Labs losses stabilise below $20B/year as smart-glasses hardware gains commercial traction; regulatory fines (DMA, GDPR) remain manageable without structural remedy"
bull_narrative: "AI-driven ad-targeting unlocks a step-change in advertiser ROAS, re-accelerating revenue growth above consensus; Reality Labs smart-glasses pivot toward profitable hardware and enterprise AR; EU/US regulatory outcomes resolved without structural separation — regulatory overhang re-rated as a non-event"
failure_modes:
  - "DAP (Daily Active People) growth decelerates below 3% YoY — engagement floor premise breaks, monetisation leverage disappears"
  - "EU antitrust forced structural separation of Messenger, WhatsApp, or Instagram from the core Facebook platform"
  - "US DOJ action seeking dissolution of the Instagram and/or WhatsApp acquisitions"
  - "Reality Labs capex sustainably above $20B/year without a credible near-term monetisation path — free cash flow drag becomes consensus-thesis-breaking"
  - "AI-generated content floods the feed and degrades ad-targeting signal quality — privacy-sandbox-style ROAS deterioration without a regulatory catalyst"
  - "Threads fails to retain users at scale; no advertising revenue materialises by 2027 — eliminates the third-pillar optionality embedded in the base case"
---

# META Playbook

**Coverage status:** ESTABLISHED · INCUMBENT  
**Thesis in one line:** Family of Apps cash machine + Reality Labs binary option + AI ad-targeting accuracy lift, with regulatory overhang as a recurring drag but not a structural break.

---

## 1. Business Architecture

### Segment mix (approximate FY2025 basis)

| Segment | Revenue share | Operating income share | Growth trajectory |
|---|---|---|---|
| Family of Apps (FoA) — advertising | ~97% | ~110%+ (absorbs RL losses) | Mid-to-high teens |
| Reality Labs (RL) | ~3% | −25% (operating loss ~$17–20B/yr) | Near-zero revenue; rising investment |

FoA advertising is the only cash-generating segment. RL is a long-duration binary bet: either smart glasses / AR / VR achieve commercial scale (2028+) or the division is wound down / spun off. The DCF base case values FoA only; RL optionality is a qualitative upside that is not modelled in the central case.

### Key revenue drivers within FoA

1. **DAP (Daily Active People)** — the volume metric. Consensus expects low-to-mid single-digit YoY growth from a ~3.4B base. Below 3% is a warning; above 5% is a positive surprise.
2. **ARPU (Average Revenue Per User)** — the monetisation metric, driven by ad price (CPMs), ad load, and AI-targeting ROAS improvements. NA/EU ARPUs are 10–20× rest-of-world; mix shift toward APAC depresses blended ARPU.
3. **AI ad-targeting** — Meta's AI-driven ad-targeting (Advantage+ suite) claims a measurable ROAS lift for advertisers. This is the swing factor in the base case: if advertisers continuously validate ROAS improvements, ad budgets shift toward Meta even at higher CPMs.

---

## 2. Capex-Cycle Position

Meta is in an **accelerating investment phase** (2024–2027). Data-centre and AI infrastructure capex is guided to $60–65B in 2025, up from $38B in 2024. This depresses FCF margin below steady-state.

**Normalised-margin thesis:** The `terminal_margin` override of 28% reflects a thesis-consistent steady-state SBC-stripped FCF margin once the current capex cycle digests (2027+). The TTM clean FCF margin (typically 22–25% at peak capex) understates this — hence the override prevents anchoring the DCF on a trough-margin base.

**Risk:** If AI capex is structural and perpetual (not cyclical), 28% terminal margin never materialises. The bear case's narrative captures this via compressed Y1 anchor.

---

## 3. Active Catalysts

| Catalyst | Probability | IV impact | Direction |
|---|---|---|---|
| EU DMA structural remedy (data-sharing mandate) | ~30% | −10–15% | Negative — share IV |
| US DOJ antitrust (Instagram/WhatsApp dissolution) | ~15% | −20–30% | Strongly negative |
| Reality Labs hardware breakthrough (smart glasses sell-out) | ~20% | +5–10% | Positive — optionality re-rating |
| AI ROAS re-acceleration above consensus | ~35% | +10–15% | Positive — bull-case trigger |
| Threads monetisation materially earlier than 2027 | ~25% | +3–5% | Positive — incremental |
| Macro recession (ad-budget freeze) | ~20% | −15–20% | Negative — all scenarios shift bear-ward |

---

## 4. Sell-Side Disagreement Axes

The real arguments the sell-side is having on META right now:

1. **Capex duration:** Is the $60B+ infrastructure spend a one-time catch-up (2024–2026) after which capex/revenue normalises ≤15%? Or is AI infrastructure a structural annual commitment that permanently compresses FCF margins? Bulls model mean-reversion; bears model a new capex regime.

2. **AI ROAS sustainability:** Is the Advantage+ ROAS lift a durable structural advantage (AI is a moat) or a cyclical tailwind that competitors (Google, TikTok) will match within 18–24 months? Bulls model permanent monetisation acceleration; bears model CPM normalisation.

3. **Regulatory endpoint:** Is the regulatory risk a repeated-fines tax (~2–3% of FCF/year) that the stock already prices in, or is structural separation (Instagram/WhatsApp dissolution) a non-trivial probability? The market's current implied probability for hard structural action appears to be <10%, which is where the disagreement sits.

4. **Reality Labs terminal value:** Is RL worth zero (a recurring cash drain that should be shut down), a negative value (capex overhang reduces equity value), a call option (worth $20–50B if spatial computing achieves scale), or a strategic platform (worth $100B+ in a 10-year horizon)? This disagreement is the largest source of cross-analyst target-price dispersion.

5. **DAP ceiling:** Does the ~3.4B DAP figure represent near-saturation in developed markets (implying ARPU must carry all growth), or is there a meaningful tail of untapped engagement in South/Southeast Asia and Africa that sustains volume growth for another 5 years?

---

## 5. Failure Modes

These are the thesis-breaking scenarios that the DCF does not capture because they are discrete rather than smooth:

- **DAP deceleration below 3% YoY** — the monetisation ARPU lever requires volume to remain relevant; sustained sub-3% DAP growth signals a platform that is mature without the ARPU ceiling to offset it.
- **EU structural separation** — a binding remedy requiring interoperability or data separation between Facebook/Instagram/WhatsApp would reduce the FoA bundle's CPM efficiency and potentially require structural divestitures at depressed multiples.
- **US acquisition reversal** — the most damaging tail risk; a forced unwinding of Instagram or WhatsApp would bifurcate cash flows, require separate capex programmes, and likely destroy synergy value.
- **Reality Labs structural capex** — if the $17–20B/year RL operating loss is not a cyclical investment but a permanent run-rate, steady-state FCF is systematically overstated in all scenarios.
- **AI-generated content flood** — if GenAI content degrades engagement quality (user fatigue, ad-adjacency concerns), the engagement-monetisation loop could weaken faster than the current data suggests.

---

## 6. Ticker-Specific Overrides (Rationale)

| Field | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `base_wacc` | 9.0% | Beta ~1.1–1.2; risk-free rate 4.2% (US 10yr); ERP ~5.5%; regulatory premium ~0.3pp. Rounds to 9.0%. |
| `growth_ceiling` | 18% | Consensus EPS growth estimate is typically 18–22% for META; 18% matches the lower bound of consensus and the conservative interpretation of mid-teens FoA revenue growth × operating leverage. Consensus takes precedence when available; this overrides the generic fallback. |
| `terminal_margin` | 28% | Capex-normalised SBC-stripped FCF margin estimate once the AI infrastructure investment cycle digests (~2027+). Current TTM clean margin (≈22–25%) is trough-distorted; anchoring Y1 FCF on the normalised margin prevents systematic understatement of the base IV during a known investment cycle. |
| `confidence_anchor` | HIGH | MCP data quality for META is consistently good (4y FCF history, SBC available, consensus estimates populated). No structural reason to cap below HIGH unless SBC or shares data degrades at run time. |

---

## 7. Historical Reset-and-Recover Priors

| Episode | Drawdown | Catalyst | Recovery | Lesson |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2018 Cambridge Analytica / GDPR | −43% peak-to-trough | Privacy scandal + regulatory reaction | Full recovery in ~12 months | Regulatory events reset the stock sharply but do not break the ad-monetisation flywheel if DAP holds |
| 2022 "Year of Efficiency" reset | −76% peak-to-trough (2021–2022) | Reality Labs losses disclosed + DAP YoY decline (Q1 2022, first ever) + macro ad recession | Recovery to all-time highs by 2023 | When Zuckerberg pivots to cost discipline, execution follows quickly; the reset creates a trough-margin base year that inflates trailing FCF CAGRs for 2–3 years afterward (this is the ABA-111 cap rationale for META) |
| 2023 AI-reset rerating | +194% in calendar year | "Year of Efficiency" cost cuts + AI ad-targeting ROAS inflection + broader AI rerating | — | The stock rewards operational discipline and AI-monetisation evidence faster than consensus updates |

**Mean-reversion implication:** META has consistently recovered from regulatory and operational resets within 12–24 months when the core DAP/ARPU flywheel remains intact. The recovery speed is higher when (a) management demonstrates cost discipline and (b) DAP does not sustain a multi-quarter decline. Bear scenarios that assume a permanent structural break (dissolution, permanent engagement loss) are qualitatively different from cyclical reset scenarios — the historical prior does not apply to a forced structural outcome.

100	
101	**Ticker mismatch** (SIGNAL OUTPUT in context is for a different ticker, AND no same-day JSON for the requested ticker exists) is treated as "no upstream Signal" — use the Step 1 refusal message.
102	
103	### ROUTE — Branch on `profit_stage` (with `--pre-profit` override)
104	
105	After the gate passes (`model_ready = YES`, or `CONDITIONAL` with `--confirm`), pick a variant:
106	
107	| `profit_stage` | `--pre-profit` flag | Variant |
108	|---|---|---|
109	| ESTABLISHED | absent | **Standard two-stage DCF** (GATHER → COMPUTE — ESTABLISHED below) |
110	| ESTABLISHED | present | **Pre-profit variant** (GATHER → COMPUTE — EMERGING below). Emit: `Forcing pre-profit variant on ESTABLISHED ticker — terminal-value DCF skipped, revenue-multiple exit + FCF inflection used instead.` |
111	| EMERGING | absent or present | **Pre-profit variant** (GATHER → COMPUTE — EMERGING below) |
112	
113	The `--pre-profit` override exists for transition-year companies (e.g. RDDT, recently profitable) where the trailing-FCF base is too thin for stable Gordon-growth terminal math, but where revenue trajectory + comp multiples + dilution schedule still produce a defensible range.
114	
115	### PLAYBOOK LOAD — Load ticker-specific overrides if present
116	
117	Runs after ROUTE and before any MCP calls. Loads per-ticker depth from `playbooks/TICKER.md` when present; falls back silently to generic logic when absent. Implementation: ABA-112.
118	
119	**Step 1 — Resolve the path.**
120	
121	`playbooks/TICKER.md` where TICKER is the uppercased, verbatim ticker symbol — preserves period-suffixed exchange codes (e.g. `playbooks/ADYEN.AS.md`).
122	
123	**Step 2 — Check existence.**
124	
125	If the file does not exist:
126	- Emit: `No playbook for TICKER — using generic ESTABLISHED/EMERGING logic.`
127	- Set `playbook_state = {loaded: false}`.
128	- Proceed to GATHER; no overrides apply.
129	
130	**Step 3 — Load and parse.**
131	
132	Read the file. Extract the YAML frontmatter (the block between the opening and closing `---` delimiters at the top of the file). Parse it.
133	
134	- **Malformed YAML** (parse error): halt with `Playbook error: playbooks/TICKER.md — YAML frontmatter could not be parsed: <error>. Fix the playbook before re-running.` Do not proceed; do not call any MCP tool.
135	- **Unknown fields** (keys not in the recognised set below): for each unknown key, emit a one-line warning — `Playbook warning: unknown field '<field>' in playbooks/TICKER.md — ignored.` — then continue. Unknown fields are forward-compatible; they do not fail the load.
136	- **Missing `last_updated` field**: use `unknown` as the date in the load line; do not fail.
137	
138	**Step 4 — Recognised frontmatter fields (all optional):**
139	
140	| Field | Type | Effect |
141	|---|---|---|
142	| `last_updated` | ISO date string | Display in load line; no DCF effect |
143	| `base_wacc` | float (e.g. `9.0`) | Pre-fills WACC; suppresses the MIP ask when the user does not also pass `--wacc=X.X` (see GATHER WACC rule below) |
144	| `growth_ceiling` | float (e.g. `0.18`) | Replaces the generic 18% / 35% fallback in COMPUTE Step 3; consensus still takes precedence over playbook when available |
145	| `terminal_margin` | float (e.g. `0.28`) | On ESTABLISHED path: replaces `fcf_margin_ttm` as the FCF margin anchor in the Year-1 FCF projection (Step 2) when TTM margin is distorted by a capex cycle. On pre-profit path: pre-fills the MIP terminal clean FCF margin target question (user still confirms). |
146	| `bear_narrative` | string | Replaces the generic bear scenario one-liner in OUTPUT; applied verbatim |
147	| `base_narrative` | string | Replaces the generic base scenario one-liner in OUTPUT; applied verbatim |
148	| `bull_narrative` | string | Replaces the generic bull scenario one-liner in OUTPUT; applied verbatim |
149	| `scenario_axes` | object | Advanced: per-scenario overrides of growth_ceiling / terminal_margin / wacc_delta for discrete catalyst structures (e.g. a binary break-up outcome vs a continuous smooth scenario). Structure: `{bear: {growth_ceiling, terminal_margin, wacc_delta}, base: {...}, bull: {...}}`. Named axes override the standard ±multiplier logic for that scenario; unspecified axes fall back to the multiplier table. |
150	| `failure_modes` | list of strings | Surfaced in OUTPUT after Position sizing as `Monitor: [item1; item2; ...]`; also recorded in `stages.model.playbook.failure_modes` |
151	| `confidence_anchor` | string (`HIGH \| MEDIUM \| LOW`) | Playbook-set max confidence; binding when stricter than the runtime-derived value |
152	
153	**Step 5 — Emit the load line.**
154	
155	When the file is present and valid:
156	
157	```
158	Playbook loaded: playbooks/TICKER.md (last updated YYYY-MM-DD). Overrides applied: [comma-separated list of recognised frontmatter fields that are present].
159	```
160	
161	If no recognised field is present (empty frontmatter or all unknown), emit:
162	```
163	Playbook loaded: playbooks/TICKER.md (last updated YYYY-MM-DD). Overrides applied: none — playbook body only.
164	```
165	
166	Set `playbook_state = {loaded: true, path: "playbooks/TICKER.md", last_updated: "YYYY-MM-DD", overrides_applied: [...]}`.
167	
168	**Step 6 — Override hierarchy.**
169	
170	Priority order (highest wins):
171	1. Runtime flag (`--wacc=X.X`) — beats playbook and MIP
172	2. Playbook frontmatter — beats generic defaults
173	3. Generic defaults (18%/35% fallback ceiling, TTM FCF margin, generic narratives)
174	
175	The user can always override playbook values at runtime. The only field the user cannot downgrade is `confidence_anchor` — a playbook cap of MEDIUM means the run caps at MEDIUM even if all other conditions would yield HIGH.
176	
177	Proceed to GATHER.
178	
179	### GATHER — DCF inputs (ESTABLISHED path)
180	
181	Run these MCP calls in this order. Every retrieved figure is held with its raw value; never round during gather. State which calls succeeded and which fell back before COMPUTE.
182	
183	1. **`get_ratios(ticker)`** — fields used: `currentPrice`, `marketCap`, `sharesOutstanding` (fallback when diluted shares missing), `eps_ttm`.
184	2. **`get_financials(ticker)`** — used for:
185	   - `years[].free_cash_flow` (4y usable per ABA-47 — accept 4y base; 5th column is dropped server-side)
186	   - `years[].revenue` (for FCF margin derivation)
187	   - `years[].stock_based_compensation` (required — stripped from FCF before margin / CAGR per ABA-110; methodology consistency with `/stock-signal` Step 5)
188	   - `years[].shares_outstanding_diluted` (latest year — primary shares figure for per-share IV)
189	   - `years[0].total_debt` and `years[0].cash` (net debt for EV→equity bridge)
190	3. **`get_estimates(ticker)`** — fields used: `ntm_revenue`, `ntm_eps`, `eps_growth_5y`. NTM revenue anchors Year-1 FCF projection (Step 2); NTM EPS is used only for sanity-check; `eps_growth_5y` is the long-term sell-side consensus growth rate, consumed as the Y2–Y5 CAGR ceiling per ABA-111 (Step 3) — may be null, in which case the 18% fallback ceiling applies.
191	
192	**Manual Input Protocol (`skills/_shared/MANUAL_INPUT_PROTOCOL.md`) fires when:**
193	
194	- `currentPrice` is null (cannot compute upside/downside)
195	- Fewer than 3 historical years of `free_cash_flow` are usable (trailing CAGR cannot be derived)
196	- `years[0].total_debt`, `years[0].cash`, or `shares_outstanding_diluted` is null
197	- `years[0].stock_based_compensation` is null — refuse with the message `SBC data unavailable; DCF cannot proceed without SBC-stripped FCF base. Use Manual Input Protocol to paste latest TTM SBC.` and ask the user via grouped paste-in for the latest TTM SBC in dollars (e.g. `20400000000` for $20.4B). Pasted SBC is tagged `source: "user_paste"` and caps `meta.confidence` at MEDIUM regardless of other inputs. If 4y SBC history is needed for `fcf_cagr_3y` and any prior year is also null, ask for the full series in the same paste-in.
198	- **base WACC** — yfinance does not expose risk-free rate, beta, or capital structure inputs that would let the skill derive WACC honestly. Resolution order:
199	  1. **`--wacc=X.X` runtime flag** (parsed in initial GATHER step 5): use directly; no ask. Record `source: "runtime_flag"`.
200	  2. **Playbook `base_wacc`** (loaded in PLAYBOOK LOAD): present to user for confirmation — `Base WACC: X.X% (from playbook — confirm or override:)`. User may accept (records `source: "playbook"`) or override (records `source: "user_paste"`, caps confidence at MEDIUM). Never silently apply without acknowledgement.
201	  3. **No playbook / no flag**: ask via grouped paste-in `base WACC %` as before. Record `source: "user_paste"`. Never default this value.
202	
203	Any field accepted via paste-in is tagged `source: "user_paste"` and added to `meta.manual_inputs`; overall `meta.confidence` caps at MEDIUM.
204	
205	### GATHER — Engagement modifier (ESTABLISHED path, opt-in)
206	
207	**Runs only when `--engagement-modifier` was passed.** When the flag is absent, skip this entire sub-section and proceed directly to COMPUTE; the `engagement_modifier` JSON block is omitted from output and no OUTPUT line is emitted.
208	
209	When the flag is present, execute the steps below. Every terminal state writes a `stages.model.engagement_modifier` block per the JSON contract in OUTPUT below — `status ∈ {applied, unavailable, no_kpi_mapping, user_skipped}`. Non-applied states must populate `status_reason` from the enum `{missing_ai_layer, no_recent_print, source_unreachable, extraction_failed, non_interactive}` (FR5, NFR3).
210	
211	**Step 1 — AI-layer gate (FR1).**
212	
213	Read `upstream_signal.ai_layer` (from the SIGNAL OUTPUT block resolved in GATE, or `stages.signal.ai_layer` in the on-disk fallback).
214	
215	- `ai_layer ∈ {APPLICATION, INCUMBENT}` → continue to Step 2.
216	- `ai_layer ∈ {INFRASTRUCTURE, FOUNDATION, NONE}` → emit `status: "unavailable", status_reason: "missing_ai_layer"` (ai_layer doesn't qualify, not strictly missing — but the enum's `missing_ai_layer` covers both "absent" and "present-but-non-qualifying" per spike-decision linkage; the audit trail records the actual value in `ai_layer_observed`). Skip to COMPUTE.
217	- `ai_layer` absent (upstream Signal predates AI-layer field) → emit `status: "unavailable", status_reason: "missing_ai_layer"`, with `ai_layer_observed: null`. Skip to COMPUTE. **Do not second-guess the upstream classification — re-run `/stock-signal` if the user believes it's wrong.**
218	
219	**Step 2 — KPI-map lookup (FR7).**
220	
221	Read `skills/_shared/engagement_kpi_map.json`. Record `kpi_map_schema_version` from the top-level field for the audit trail (Task 8 ADR D5).
222	
223	- Ticker is in `tickers` → take the `primary_kpi` (and `evidence.exhibit_url` as a starting hint) and continue to Step 3.
224	- Ticker is in `excluded_tickers` → emit `status: "no_kpi_mapping"`, with `excluded_reason` copied from the map's `reason` field. Skip to COMPUTE.
225	- Ticker is absent from both → emit `status: "no_kpi_mapping"`, `status_reason: null` (the enum doesn't cover "ticker not mapped" because that's a `status`, not a reason). Skip to COMPUTE.
226	
227	**Step 3 — Locate the latest 8-K Ex 99.1 (FR8 source resolution order).**
228	
229	1. Try EDGAR MCP `get_8k_exhibit(ticker, latest=true)` if the server is reachable. Returns the Exhibit 99.1 content + filing date + accession.
230	2. Fallback: direct EDGAR HTTP. Resolve the latest 8-K via the submissions API (`https://data.sec.gov/submissions/CIK<10digit>.json`), traverse `recent.accessionNumber` + `recent.primaryDocument` to find the Ex 99.1 URL. SEC requires a custom User-Agent header (`Stock Review Skill-Pack <email>`); use it on every request.
231	3. If both the EDGAR MCP and the direct HTTP path fail (network error, 5xx persisting past one retry with 2 s backoff per design doc §316) → emit `status: "unavailable", status_reason: "source_unreachable"`. Skip to COMPUTE.
232	4. If the latest 8-K is older than 90 days (mid-quarter run between earnings) → fall through to `WebSearch` for the most recent earnings press release. Mark the audit trail with `source_type: "websearch_fallback"`. If the WebSearch returns no plausible recent release → `status: "unavailable", status_reason: "no_recent_print"`.
233	
234	**Step 4 — Extract the KPI value and YoY.**
235	
236	Use the `primary_kpi.source_phrase` from the map as the regex anchor. Pull the latest-period value AND the YoY-prior-period value from the Ex 99.1 document (most press releases disclose both in the same sentence: *"DAP increased 6% year over year to 3.43 billion"*). Compute `yoy_change = (current / prior_year) - 1` from the two raw numbers — do not trust a stated "%" without numerical cross-check.
237	
238	If extraction fails (regex doesn't match, or numbers are non-numeric, or YoY can't be derived) → emit `status: "unavailable", status_reason: "extraction_failed"`. Skip to COMPUTE.
239	
240	**Step 5 — Classify direction × magnitude per pre-registered constants** (ADR `engagement-modifier-constants.md`, C1/C2):
241	
242	- `abs(yoy_change) < 0.02` → `direction = 0`, `magnitude = "neutral"`. The modifier is a no-op (`base_anchor_multiplier = 1.00`). Status is still `applied` — the audit trail records that the modifier ran and concluded "no signal."
243	- `abs(yoy_change) >= 0.02 AND abs(yoy_change) < 0.08` → `direction = sign(yoy_change)`, `magnitude = "mild"`.
244	- `abs(yoy_change) >= 0.08` → `direction = sign(yoy_change)`, `magnitude = "strong"`.
245	
246	**Step 5b — Revision-direction agreement guardrail** (spike-decision §"Lead vs confirm").
247	
248	Fetch Yahoo Finance's live `/analysis/` page to read the trailing 30-day EPS revision direction. The modifier is reframed as a **confirmer** (not a predictor): when engagement direction agrees with the post-print revision direction the market has already begun to record, the modifier applies; when they strictly disagree, the modifier suppresses to neutral.
249	
250	1. **Fetch.** `WebFetch https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/<TICKER>/analysis/`. One retry with 2 s backoff on transient 5xx / network error (mirrors design-doc §316).
251	
252	2. **Extract EPS Trend.** Locate the "EPS Trend" table. Read the **Next Year** column's "Current Estimate" and "30 Days Ago" rows. Compute `revision_pct = (current - thirty_days_ago) / thirty_days_ago`. Direction: `+1` if `revision_pct > 0`, `-1` if `< 0`, `0` if exactly zero.
253	
254	3. **Fetch / parse failure** (HTTP error after retry, page DOM changed, regex miss, non-numeric values) → set `revision: {metric: "eps_ntm_30d", revision_pct: null, direction: null, source_url: "<attempted URL>"}` and `agreement: null`. Apply the modifier per C3/C4 unchanged — this is the legacy "revision-data unavailable" fall-through. Proceed to Step 6.
255	
256	4. **Compute agreement.** `agreement = (revision.direction == direction) OR (revision.direction == 0) OR (direction == 0)`. A neutral side (KPI deadband or zero revision) is treated as compatible — there's no signal to disagree with.
257	
258	5. **`agreement == false` (strict disagreement).** Emit `status: "direction_disagreement"`, `base_anchor_multiplier = 1.00`, `clamped_from = null`, `user_confirmed: false`. Populate the audit trail fully (kpi block, revision block, `agreement: false`). **Skip Step 6 (MIP)** — there is no modifier to confirm. Proceed to COMPUTE; the base scenario is unperturbed (Step 2b becomes a no-op; Step 6b does not run).
259	
260	6. **`agreement == true`** (or revision unavailable per case 3) → preserve `base_anchor_multiplier` from Step 5 and continue to Step 6 (MIP).
261	
262	7. **Forward-log emission (Task 10b).** Whether the modifier applies, suppresses, or falls through, append one line to `tests/fixtures/engagement_kpi/forward_log.jsonl` (create the file with mode 0644 if absent):
263	
264	   ```jsonl
265	   {"ticker": "<T>", "period": "<Q1 2026>", "engagement_direction": -1|0|1, "revision_direction_30d": -1|0|1|null, "agreement": true|false|null, "run_ts": "<ISO8601 UTC>"}
266	   ```
267	
268	   Append-only. Never edited, rotated, or culled. This is the dataset NFR7 (≥60% direction agreement on n≥24) is evaluated against once enough forward samples accumulate. Per spike-decision §"NFR7 — deferred, not deleted", the log records the pre-suppression decision too — `engagement_direction` is the Step-5 KPI direction, not the post-guardrail multiplier sign — so the accumulated dataset preserves the engagement-only direction-agreement evidence for the eventual NFR7 evaluation. Emission failures (disk full, permission denied) **never** block the model run — log a warning and proceed; the forward log is telemetry, not a contract artefact.
269	
270	**Step 6 — Manual Input Protocol confirmation (FR2, NFR5).**
271	
272	Skip this step entirely when Step 5b emitted `status: "direction_disagreement"` (modifier already suppressed).
273	
274	Present the extracted values to the user via the grouped paste-in pattern:
275	
276	```
277	ENGAGEMENT MODIFIER — confirm before applying:
278	  Ticker:        META
279	  KPI:           DAP (Family Daily Active People)

file.content:
**Ticker mismatch** (SIGNAL OUTPUT in context is for a different ticker, AND no same-day JSON for the requested ticker exists) is treated as "no upstream Signal" — use the Step 1 refusal message.

### ROUTE — Branch on `profit_stage` (with `--pre-profit` override)

After the gate passes (`model_ready = YES`, or `CONDITIONAL` with `--confirm`), pick a variant:

| `profit_stage` | `--pre-profit` flag | Variant |
|---|---|---|
| ESTABLISHED | absent | **Standard two-stage DCF** (GATHER → COMPUTE — ESTABLISHED below) |
| ESTABLISHED | present | **Pre-profit variant** (GATHER → COMPUTE — EMERGING below). Emit: `Forcing pre-profit variant on ESTABLISHED ticker — terminal-value DCF skipped, revenue-multiple exit + FCF inflection used instead.` |
| EMERGING | absent or present | **Pre-profit variant** (GATHER → COMPUTE — EMERGING below) |

The `--pre-profit` override exists for transition-year companies (e.g. RDDT, recently profitable) where the trailing-FCF base is too thin for stable Gordon-growth terminal math, but where revenue trajectory + comp multiples + dilution schedule still produce a defensible range.

### PLAYBOOK LOAD — Load ticker-specific overrides if present

Runs after ROUTE and before any MCP calls. Loads per-ticker depth from `playbooks/TICKER.md` when present; falls back silently to generic logic when absent. Implementation: ABA-112.

**Step 1 — Resolve the path.**

`playbooks/TICKER.md` where TICKER is the uppercased, verbatim ticker symbol — preserves period-suffixed exchange codes (e.g. `playbooks/ADYEN.AS.md`).

**Step 2 — Check existence.**

If the file does not exist:
- Emit: `No playbook for TICKER — using generic ESTABLISHED/EMERGING logic.`
- Set `playbook_state = {loaded: false}`.
- Proceed to GATHER; no overrides apply.

**Step 3 — Load and parse.**

Read the file. Extract the YAML frontmatter (the block between the opening and closing `---` delimiters at the top of the file). Parse it.

- **Malformed YAML** (parse error): halt with `Playbook error: playbooks/TICKER.md — YAML frontmatter could not be parsed: <error>. Fix the playbook before re-running.` Do not proceed; do not call any MCP tool.
- **Unknown fields** (keys not in the recognised set below): for each unknown key, emit a one-line warning — `Playbook warning: unknown field '<field>' in playbooks/TICKER.md — ignored.` — then continue. Unknown fields are forward-compatible; they do not fail the load.
- **Missing `last_updated` field**: use `unknown` as the date in the load line; do not fail.

**Step 4 — Recognised frontmatter fields (all optional):**

| Field | Type | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| `last_updated` | ISO date string | Display in load line; no DCF effect |
| `base_wacc` | float (e.g. `9.0`) | Pre-fills WACC; suppresses the MIP ask when the user does not also pass `--wacc=X.X` (see GATHER WACC rule below) |
| `growth_ceiling` | float (e.g. `0.18`) | Replaces the generic 18% / 35% fallback in COMPUTE Step 3; consensus still takes precedence over playbook when available |
| `terminal_margin` | float (e.g. `0.28`) | On ESTABLISHED path: replaces `fcf_margin_ttm` as the FCF margin anchor in the Year-1 FCF projection (Step 2) when TTM margin is distorted by a capex cycle. On pre-profit path: pre-fills the MIP terminal clean FCF margin target question (user still confirms). |
| `bear_narrative` | string | Replaces the generic bear scenario one-liner in OUTPUT; applied verbatim |
| `base_narrative` | string | Replaces the generic base scenario one-liner in OUTPUT; applied verbatim |
| `bull_narrative` | string | Replaces the generic bull scenario one-liner in OUTPUT; applied verbatim |
| `scenario_axes` | object | Advanced: per-scenario overrides of growth_ceiling / terminal_margin / wacc_delta for discrete catalyst structures (e.g. a binary break-up outcome vs a continuous smooth scenario). Structure: `{bear: {growth_ceiling, terminal_margin, wacc_delta}, base: {...}, bull: {...}}`. Named axes override the standard ±multiplier logic for that scenario; unspecified axes fall back to the multiplier table. |
| `failure_modes` | list of strings | Surfaced in OUTPUT after Position sizing as `Monitor: [item1; item2; ...]`; also recorded in `stages.model.playbook.failure_modes` |
| `confidence_anchor` | string (`HIGH \| MEDIUM \| LOW`) | Playbook-set max confidence; binding when stricter than the runtime-derived value |

**Step 5 — Emit the load line.**

When the file is present and valid:

```
Playbook loaded: playbooks/TICKER.md (last updated YYYY-MM-DD). Overrides applied: [comma-separated list of recognised frontmatter fields that are present].
```

If no recognised field is present (empty frontmatter or all unknown), emit:
```
Playbook loaded: playbooks/TICKER.md (last updated YYYY-MM-DD). Overrides applied: none — playbook body only.
```

Set `playbook_state = {loaded: true, path: "playbooks/TICKER.md", last_updated: "YYYY-MM-DD", overrides_applied: [...]}`.

**Step 6 — Override hierarchy.**

Priority order (highest wins):
1. Runtime flag (`--wacc=X.X`) — beats playbook and MIP
2. Playbook frontmatter — beats generic defaults
3. Generic defaults (18%/35% fallback ceiling, TTM FCF margin, generic narratives)

The user can always override playbook values at runtime. The only field the user cannot downgrade is `confidence_anchor` — a playbook cap of MEDIUM means the run caps at MEDIUM even if all other conditions would yield HIGH.

Proceed to GATHER.

### GATHER — DCF inputs (ESTABLISHED path)

Run these MCP calls in this order. Every retrieved figure is held with its raw value; never round during gather. State which calls succeeded and which fell back before COMPUTE.

1. **`get_ratios(ticker)`** — fields used: `currentPrice`, `marketCap`, `sharesOutstanding` (fallback when diluted shares missing), `eps_ttm`.
2. **`get_financials(ticker)`** — used for:
   - `years[].free_cash_flow` (4y usable per ABA-47 — accept 4y base; 5th column is dropped server-side)
   - `years[].revenue` (for FCF margin derivation)
   - `years[].stock_based_compensation` (required — stripped from FCF before margin / CAGR per ABA-110; methodology consistency with `/stock-signal` Step 5)
   - `years[].shares_outstanding_diluted` (latest year — primary shares figure for per-share IV)
   - `years[0].total_debt` and `years[0].cash` (net debt for EV→equity bridge)
3. **`get_estimates(ticker)`** — fields used: `ntm_revenue`, `ntm_eps`, `eps_growth_5y`. NTM revenue anchors Year-1 FCF projection (Step 2); NTM EPS is used only for sanity-check; `eps_growth_5y` is the long-term sell-side consensus growth rate, consumed as the Y2–Y5 CAGR ceiling per ABA-111 (Step 3) — may be null, in which case the 18% fallback ceiling applies.

**Manual Input Protocol (`skills/_shared/MANUAL_INPUT_PROTOCOL.md`) fires when:**

- `currentPrice` is null (cannot compute upside/downside)
- Fewer than 3 historical years of `free_cash_flow` are usable (trailing CAGR cannot be derived)
- `years[0].total_debt`, `years[0].cash`, or `shares_outstanding_diluted` is null
- `years[0].stock_based_compensation` is null — refuse with the message `SBC data unavailable; DCF cannot proceed without SBC-stripped FCF base. Use Manual Input Protocol to paste latest TTM SBC.` and ask the user via grouped paste-in for the latest TTM SBC in dollars (e.g. `20400000000` for $20.4B). Pasted SBC is tagged `source: "user_paste"` and caps `meta.confidence` at MEDIUM regardless of other inputs. If 4y SBC history is needed for `fcf_cagr_3y` and any prior year is also null, ask for the full series in the same paste-in.
- **base WACC** — yfinance does not expose risk-free rate, beta, or capital structure inputs that would let the skill derive WACC honestly. Resolution order:
  1. **`--wacc=X.X` runtime flag** (parsed in initial GATHER step 5): use directly; no ask. Record `source: "runtime_flag"`.
  2. **Playbook `base_wacc`** (loaded in PLAYBOOK LOAD): present to user for confirmation — `Base WACC: X.X% (from playbook — confirm or override:)`. User may accept (records `source: "playbook"`) or override (records `source: "user_paste"`, caps confidence at MEDIUM). Never silently apply without acknowledgement.
  3. **No playbook / no flag**: ask via grouped paste-in `base WACC %` as before. Record `source: "user_paste"`. Never default this value.

Any field accepted via paste-in is tagged `source: "user_paste"` and added to `meta.manual_inputs`; overall `meta.confidence` caps at MEDIUM.

### GATHER — Engagement modifier (ESTABLISHED path, opt-in)

**Runs only when `--engagement-modifier` was passed.** When the flag is absent, skip this entire sub-section and proceed directly to COMPUTE; the `engagement_modifier` JSON block is omitted from output and no OUTPUT line is emitted.

When the flag is present, execute the steps below. Every terminal state writes a `stages.model.engagement_modifier` block per the JSON contract in OUTPUT below — `status ∈ {applied, unavailable, no_kpi_mapping, user_skipped}`. Non-applied states must populate `status_reason` from the enum `{missing_ai_layer, no_recent_print, source_unreachable, extraction_failed, non_interactive}` (FR5, NFR3).

**Step 1 — AI-layer gate (FR1).**

Read `upstream_signal.ai_layer` (from the SIGNAL OUTPUT block resolved in GATE, or `stages.signal.ai_layer` in the on-disk fallback).

- `ai_layer ∈ {APPLICATION, INCUMBENT}` → continue to Step 2.
- `ai_layer ∈ {INFRASTRUCTURE, FOUNDATION, NONE}` → emit `status: "unavailable", status_reason: "missing_ai_layer"` (ai_layer doesn't qualify, not strictly missing — but the enum's `missing_ai_layer` covers both "absent" and "present-but-non-qualifying" per spike-decision linkage; the audit trail records the actual value in `ai_layer_observed`). Skip to COMPUTE.
- `ai_layer` absent (upstream Signal predates AI-layer field) → emit `status: "unavailable", status_reason: "missing_ai_layer"`, with `ai_layer_observed: null`. Skip to COMPUTE. **Do not second-guess the upstream classification — re-run `/stock-signal` if the user believes it's wrong.**

**Step 2 — KPI-map lookup (FR7).**

Read `skills/_shared/engagement_kpi_map.json`. Record `kpi_map_schema_version` from the top-level field for the audit trail (Task 8 ADR D5).

- Ticker is in `tickers` → take the `primary_kpi` (and `evidence.exhibit_url` as a starting hint) and continue to Step 3.
- Ticker is in `excluded_tickers` → emit `status: "no_kpi_mapping"`, with `excluded_reason` copied from the map's `reason` field. Skip to COMPUTE.
- Ticker is absent from both → emit `status: "no_kpi_mapping"`, `status_reason: null` (the enum doesn't cover "ticker not mapped" because that's a `status`, not a reason). Skip to COMPUTE.

**Step 3 — Locate the latest 8-K Ex 99.1 (FR8 source resolution order).**

1. Try EDGAR MCP `get_8k_exhibit(ticker, latest=true)` if the server is reachable. Returns the Exhibit 99.1 content + filing date + accession.
2. Fallback: direct EDGAR HTTP. Resolve the latest 8-K via the submissions API (`https://data.sec.gov/submissions/CIK<10digit>.json`), traverse `recent.accessionNumber` + `recent.primaryDocument` to find the Ex 99.1 URL. SEC requires a custom User-Agent header (`Stock Review Skill-Pack <email>`); use it on every request.
3. If both the EDGAR MCP and the direct HTTP path fail (network error, 5xx persisting past one retry with 2 s backoff per design doc §316) → emit `status: "unavailable", status_reason: "source_unreachable"`. Skip to COMPUTE.
4. If the latest 8-K is older than 90 days (mid-quarter run between earnings) → fall through to `WebSearch` for the most recent earnings press release. Mark the audit trail with `source_type: "websearch_fallback"`. If the WebSearch returns no plausible recent release → `status: "unavailable", status_reason: "no_recent_print"`.

**Step 4 — Extract the KPI value and YoY.**

Use the `primary_kpi.source_phrase` from the map as the regex anchor. Pull the latest-period value AND the YoY-prior-period value from the Ex 99.1 document (most press releases disclose both in the same sentence: *"DAP increased 6% year over year to 3.43 billion"*). Compute `yoy_change = (current / prior_year) - 1` from the two raw numbers — do not trust a stated "%" without numerical cross-check.

If extraction fails (regex doesn't match, or numbers are non-numeric, or YoY can't be derived) → emit `status: "unavailable", status_reason: "extraction_failed"`. Skip to COMPUTE.

**Step 5 — Classify direction × magnitude per pre-registered constants** (ADR `engagement-modifier-constants.md`, C1/C2):

- `abs(yoy_change) < 0.02` → `direction = 0`, `magnitude = "neutral"`. The modifier is a no-op (`base_anchor_multiplier = 1.00`). Status is still `applied` — the audit trail records that the modifier ran and concluded "no signal."
- `abs(yoy_change) >= 0.02 AND abs(yoy_change) < 0.08` → `direction = sign(yoy_change)`, `magnitude = "mild"`.
- `abs(yoy_change) >= 0.08` → `direction = sign(yoy_change)`, `magnitude = "strong"`.

**Step 5b — Revision-direction agreement guardrail** (spike-decision §"Lead vs confirm").

Fetch Yahoo Finance's live `/analysis/` page to read the trailing 30-day EPS revision direction. The modifier is reframed as a **confirmer** (not a predictor): when engagement direction agrees with the post-print revision direction the market has already begun to record, the modifier applies; when they strictly disagree, the modifier suppresses to neutral.

1. **Fetch.** `WebFetch https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/<TICKER>/analysis/`. One retry with 2 s backoff on transient 5xx / network error (mirrors design-doc §316).

2. **Extract EPS Trend.** Locate the "EPS Trend" table. Read the **Next Year** column's "Current Estimate" and "30 Days Ago" rows. Compute `revision_pct = (current - thirty_days_ago) / thirty_days_ago`. Direction: `+1` if `revision_pct > 0`, `-1` if `< 0`, `0` if exactly zero.

3. **Fetch / parse failure** (HTTP error after retry, page DOM changed, regex miss, non-numeric values) → set `revision: {metric: "eps_ntm_30d", revision_pct: null, direction: null, source_url: "<attempted URL>"}` and `agreement: null`. Apply the modifier per C3/C4 unchanged — this is the legacy "revision-data unavailable" fall-through. Proceed to Step 6.

4. **Compute agreement.** `agreement = (revision.direction == direction) OR (revision.direction == 0) OR (direction == 0)`. A neutral side (KPI deadband or zero revision) is treated as compatible — there's no signal to disagree with.

5. **`agreement == false` (strict disagreement).** Emit `status: "direction_disagreement"`, `base_anchor_multiplier = 1.00`, `clamped_from = null`, `user_confirmed: false`. Populate the audit trail fully (kpi block, revision block, `agreement: false`). **Skip Step 6 (MIP)** — there is no modifier to confirm. Proceed to COMPUTE; the base scenario is unperturbed (Step 2b becomes a no-op; Step 6b does not run).

6. **`agreement == true`** (or revision unavailable per case 3) → preserve `base_anchor_multiplier` from Step 5 and continue to Step 6 (MIP).

7. **Forward-log emission (Task 10b).** Whether the modifier applies, suppresses, or falls through, append one line to `tests/fixtures/engagement_kpi/forward_log.jsonl` (create the file with mode 0644 if absent):

   ```jsonl
   {"ticker": "<T>", "period": "<Q1 2026>", "engagement_direction": -1|0|1, "revision_direction_30d": -1|0|1|null, "agreement": true|false|null, "run_ts": "<ISO8601 UTC>"}
   ```

   Append-only. Never edited, rotated, or culled. This is the dataset NFR7 (≥60% direction agreement on n≥24) is evaluated against once enough forward samples accumulate. Per spike-decision §"NFR7 — deferred, not deleted", the log records the pre-suppression decision too — `engagement_direction` is the Step-5 KPI direction, not the post-guardrail multiplier sign — so the accumulated dataset preserves the engagement-only direction-agreement evidence for the eventual NFR7 evaluation. Emission failures (disk full, permission denied) **never** block the model run — log a warning and proceed; the forward log is telemetry, not a contract artefact.

**Step 6 — Manual Input Protocol confirmation (FR2, NFR5).**

Skip this step entirely when Step 5b emitted `status: "direction_disagreement"` (modifier already suppressed).

Present the extracted values to the user via the grouped paste-in pattern:

```
ENGAGEMENT MODIFIER — confirm before applying:
  Ticker:        META
  KPI:           DAP (Family Daily Active People)

content.text: I have everything I need. The structural template is clear (mirror ASML/META), the loader contract is well-defined, and the GOOG signal data is current. Let me write the plan.

input.content:
# ABA-117 — Write playbook: GOOG

## Context

The playbook loader (ABA-112) landed at 51646d2 and the ASML/META precedents are committed. GOOG is the next planned playbook under the "depth over breadth" charter: generic INCUMBENT/ESTABLISHED defaults don't engage with the AI-search-disruption debate, the DOJ remedies uncertainty, or the Cloud margin-ramp question — and those three are exactly what drives the live IV dispersion on GOOG.

The current `/stock-signal` output (`reports/GOOG_20260517.json`) classifies GOOG as ESTABLISHED · INCUMBENT, verdict CAUTION, MODEL_READY=NO because PEG 5.76 exceeds the INCUMBENT WATCH threshold. CAUTION is purely valuation-driven on a conservative 5y CAGR fallback (5.5%; `eps_growth_5y` unavailable from yfinance). The post-ABA-132 PEG floor at 8% CAGR would re-classify this to PEG ≈ 3.96 → MODEL_READY=YES on next signal run — but the playbook is the override layer, written once and consumed every run regardless of signal verdict, so the playbook does not block on this.

Success criterion (verbatim from roadmap and the Linear issue):

> `playbooks/GOOG.md` written; `/stock-model GOOG` reflects overrides; sell-side disagreement axes are surfaced in output.

Appetite: 2–3 slices.

---

## Approach

Mirror the ASML and META playbook structures exactly. The loader contract (skills/stock-model/SKILL.md:115–177) is fixed — YAML frontmatter parses recognised fields; body is human-reference content. Use ASML.md as the structural template for length and depth, META.md as the template for how to handle discrete regulatory/structural tail risks (META encodes them as `failure_modes` rather than `scenario_axes` because they're binary not continuous).

### Slice plan

**Slice 1 — `playbooks/GOOG.md` draft with frontmatter + body**
- File: `/Users/anton/src/stock-review/playbooks/GOOG.md` (new)
- Frontmatter fields (proposed values with rationale below):
  - `ticker: GOOG`
  - `last_updated: 2026-05-18`
  - `base_wacc: 9.0`
  - `growth_ceiling: 0.13`
  - `terminal_margin: 0.30`
  - `confidence_anchor: MEDIUM`
  - `bear_narrative` / `base_narrative` / `bull_narrative` — three thesis-grounded one-liners
  - `failure_modes` — list of 5–6 discrete thesis-breaking scenarios
  - `scenario_axes` — bear: `growth_ceiling: 0.05` (AI-eats-search compression); bull: `growth_ceiling: 0.18` (Search-retains + Cloud-accelerates)
- Body sections (mirroring ASML.md §1–§7):
  1. **Business Architecture** — segment mix (Google Services / Cloud / Other Bets) + key revenue drivers (paid clicks × CPC, YouTube ads + subs, Cloud GCP/Workspace) + end-market exposure. Note: engagement KPI gap is known per `skills/_shared/engagement_kpi_map.json:87–91` (OQ6) — playbook acknowledges Search-CPM/click absolutes are not disclosed.
  2. **AI-cycle position** (substitutes for ASML's "Capex-cycle position") — where Alphabet sits in the AI capex cycle (~$91B FY2025 capex, ~70% AI-attributed per public guidance); why TTM clean FCF margin is depressed; what normalised steady-state margin looks like.
  3. **Active catalysts** — table: catalyst, probability, IV impact, direction. Includes DOJ remedy finalisation, Cloud margin print, Gemini consumer-adoption milestone, AI-Mode Search query mix shift, Waymo monetisation, AdTech case ruling.
  4. **Sell-side disagreement axes** — five live arguments: (i) AI search-query share migration (Perplexity/ChatGPT/AI-native takeoff curve), (ii) Cloud margin trajectory vs AWS/Azure peers, (iii) AI capex efficiency (compounding vs compression), (iv) DOJ remedies severity (default-payment ban / Chrome divestiture / AdTech remedy), (v) YouTube Shorts CPM convergence with in-stream. **This is the primary deliverable for the success criterion.**
  5. **Failure modes** — discrete thesis-breaking scenarios: DOJ-ordered structural separation (Chrome or AdTech), Search query-share loss crossing critical threshold (e.g., >15% migration in 24 months), AI capex returns permanently below cost-of-capital, YouTube Shorts monetisation never closes vs in-stream, Cloud growth stalls to ≤AWS, regulatory action on Android distribution outside US.
  6. **Ticker-specific overrides (rationale)** — table justifying each frontmatter field.
  7. **Historical reset-and-recover priors** — 3 episodes (2022 ad recession + privacy reset; 2023 Bing+ChatGPT scare reaction; 2024–2025 DOJ verdict drawdown). Mean-reversion implication: GOOG recovers from regulatory/competitive scares faster when Cloud growth is intact and Search query share holds; the recovery is structurally different when the failure mode is permanent (e.g., forced divestiture).

**Slice 2 — Verify `/stock-model GOOG` loads the playbook and surfaces overrides**
- Read-only check: trace through `skills/stock-model/SKILL.md` PLAYBOOK LOAD step (lines 115–177) mentally with the new frontmatter. Confirm:
  - `Overrides applied: base_wacc, growth_ceiling, terminal_margin, confidence_anchor, bear_narrative, base_narrative, bull_narrative, failure_modes, scenario_axes` will print
  - `confidence_anchor: MEDIUM` will cap meta.confidence at MEDIUM regardless of runtime-derived value
  - `scenario_axes.bear.growth_ceiling: 0.05` and `scenario_axes.bull.growth_ceiling: 0.18` override the standard ±multiplier
  - `failure_modes` will surface in OUTPUT as `Monitor: [...]`
- No code or skill-file changes expected — the loader was built generic in ABA-112.

**Slice 3 — Commit + Linear close-out**

Mirror the ASML and META playbook structures exactly. The loader contract (skills/stock-model/SKILL.md:115–177) is fixed — YAML frontmatter parses recognised fields; body is human-reference content.   - `bear_narrative` / `base_narrative` / `bull_narrative` — three thesis-grounded one-liners

  - `scenario_axes` — bear: `growth_ceiling: 0.05` (AI-eats-search compression); bull: `growth_ceiling: 0.18` (Search-retains + Cloud-accelerates)

Note: engagement KPI gap is known per `skills/_shared/engagement_kpi_map.json:87–91` (OQ6) — playbook acknowledges Search-CPM/click absolutes are not disclosed.
  2. **AI-cycle position** (substitutes for ASML's "Capex-cycle position") — where Alphabet sits in the AI capex cycle (~$91B FY2025 capex, ~70% AI-attributed per public guidance); why TTM clean FCF margin is depressed; what normalised steady-state margin looks like.
  3. **Active catalysts** — table: catalyst, probability, IV impact, direction. Includes DOJ remedy finalisation, Cloud margin print, Gemini consumer-adoption milestone, AI-Mode Search query mix shift, Waymo monetisation, AdTech case ruling.
  4. **Sell-side disagreement axes** — five live arguments: (i) AI search-query share migration (Perplexity/ChatGPT/AI-native takeoff curve), (ii) Cloud margin trajectory vs AWS/Azure peers, (iii) AI capex efficiency (compounding vs compression), (iv) DOJ remedies severity (default-payment ban / Chrome divestiture / AdTech remedy), (v) YouTube Shorts CPM convergence with in-stream. **This is the primary deliverable for the success criterion.**

End-to-end check (read-only — no model re-run required by the success criterion, only that the playbook is wired):

1. **YAML parse check** — confirm the frontmatter is valid YAML with all recognised fields (mental walk-through of loader Step 3 in `skills/stock-model/SKILL.md:130–137`).
2. **Override-list trace** — confirm the load-line `Overrides applied:` list would enumerate every populated frontmatter field per loader Step 5 (`skills/stock-model/SKILL.md:155–166`).

End-to-end runtime check is optional but recommended after merge:

This is a side-check on the **loader**, which is ABA-112's responsibility — if anything breaks, the bug is in the loader, not the playbook.

2	  "branch": "main",

7	    {

11	      "body": "1. Define observable evidence for “AI search disruption”

Right now the central thesis risk is still too narrative-driven.

The playbook correctly identifies AI-driven Search disruption as the key valuation variable, but it does not define:

what measurable indicators would confirm deterioration,
what thresholds matter,
or how to distinguish experimentation from structural behaviour change.

At minimum, the playbook should clarify:

which indicators matter most,
what constitutes temporary noise vs thesis impairment,
and what level of search-share or monetisation erosion becomes structurally concerning.

Otherwise the core debate remains descriptive rather than model-actionable.

2. Separate “Search pressure” from “Search obsolescence”

The current bear framing may understate downside severity by implicitly assuming Google Search remains the dominant interface layer.

There is an important distinction between:

slower Search growth,
vs
AI assistants replacing Search as the primary discovery/navigation layer.

Those produce fundamentally different terminal economics and should not be collapsed into a single disruption narrative.

The playbook should explicitly distinguish:

monetisation compression,
query-share migration,
and interface displacement.

These are different failure paths with different implications.

3. Strengthen the AI capex / margin thesis

The current terminal-margin logic assumes AI-related margin pressure is temporary.

That assumption needs stronger justification.

The unresolved issue is whether AI:

eventually restores operating leverage,
or
permanently increases serving costs while reducing monetisation density.

This is one of the most consequential valuation uncertainties in the entire GOOG thesis and deserves sharper articulation in the playbook rationale.

Medium Priority / Important Refinements
4. Tighten the Cloud-offset narrative

The playbook currently leans somewhat heavily on Cloud margin expansion offsetting Search uncertainty.

That should be framed more cautiously.

Key unresolved question:

is GCP developing durable economic differentiation,
or
simply participating in an increasingly capital-intensive AI infrastructure cycle?

The playbook does not need deep peer analysis, but it should acknowledge that Cloud may not fully compensate for Search deterioration.

5. Clarify that historical recoveries may not generalise

The “reset-and-recover” section risks implying prior regulatory and competitive scares are reliable precedents.

The playbook should explicitly state:
past recoveries occurred while user behaviour remained structurally intact.

AI-driven interface changes may represent a different category of risk altogether.

That caveat matters.

6. Make the regulatory section more structural

Current regulatory framing focuses mostly on remedies and legal outcomes.

More emphasis should be placed on:

weakening Google’s distribution advantages,
default-placement economics,
browser leverage,
and ecosystem control.

Those are likely more economically important than fines themselves.

Lower Priority / Optional Improvements
7. Consider explicitly categorising AI-disruption layers

This may improve clarity substantially.

Suggested separation:

interface disruption,
monetisation disruption,
cost disruption,
ecosystem displacement.

This would make the disagreement axes cleaner and reduce conceptual overlap.

8. Keep operational telemetry out of the playbook

Avoid turning the playbook into a KPI-monitoring system.

Detailed tracking of:

session behaviour,
CPC telemetry,
inference economics,
engagement instrumentation

belongs in research workflows or dashboards, not the core playbook.

The playbook should remain focused on:

durable valuation assumptions,
structural debates,
and thesis-breaking conditions.",
